author: PC Gamer
text:
Leaked Xbox Elite 3 controller images show at least one oddball addition, but it'll struggle to compete in a radically changed market
Gamepads are better, and cheaper, than they've ever been.

author: PC Gamer
text:
A PvE-only mode is coming to Marathon as Bungie lays out plans for seasons 3, 4 and 5
"We want to evolve Marathon as a whole."

author: PC Gamer
text:
The original Doom soundtrack joins 'our national playlist' in the US Library of Congress
Bobby Prince's groundbreaking soundtrack is one of 25 recordings inducted into the famed Library of Congress today.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Subnautica 2 sells 1 million copies in its first hour, and I have to imagine the CEO of Krafton is pouring himself a very stiff drink right about now
The scenario that Krafton humiliated itself to avoid seems to be coming true very quickly.

author: PC Gamer
text:
2011 was packed with iconic games: Can you guess which ones we reviewed from a quote and the score?
Even if you weren't playing them, you probably heard about them.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Windrose hits 2 million sales milestone, proving yet again that the world really, really wanted a good pirate game
Low-hanging fruit, and someone finally plucked it.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Don't sleep on Battlestar Galactica: Scattered Hopes—it's tricky, tactical and the best FTL-like since, well, FTL
So say we all.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Subnautica 2 has sold 2 million copies in 12 hours, with 9x the players as the first, and at this point I figure that Krafton CEO is already on his second bottle
Let's sea how this all plays out.

author: PC Gamer
text:
If Subnautica 2 won't let you select 4K resolution, there's a remarkably easy fix
Let's keep things simple, yes?

author: PC Gamer
text:
How to get Lucifer Rotsac in Subnautica 2
You'll need this deep-sea orange to make rubber.

author: PC Gamer
text:
How to clear an Angel Comb in Subnautica 2
Blast the viral growths to get yourself the Heat Tolerance Adaptation.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Where to find all Forza Horizon 6 Treasure Cars
Track down these nine hidden gems using just a photo.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Where to find all Forza Horizon 6 Barn Finds
Dig up these 15 old cars in rundown barns.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Owner of $400,000 in bitcoin was locked out of their account a decade ago and they just used Claude to get back in
They technically could have saved money by not cashing out earlier.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Twenty years ago, copper was king for PC enthusiasts so Asus has brought it back for its ROG anniversary. Well, sort of
All that glitters is not gold. Or copper, for that matter.

author: PC Gamer
text:
How to investigate the Tadpole Pens in Subnautica 2
Track down Zip's black box in the Hot Caves to get the door key code.

author: PC Gamer
text:
15% of PC Gamer readers are still rawdogging their desktops, while 43% of you are wholesome full desk pad people
Do you use a mouse mat while gaming?

author: PC Gamer
text:
Windrose hits 2 million sales milestone, proving yet again that the world really, really wanted a good pirate game
Low-hanging fruit, and someone finally plucked it.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Intel's 270K Plus is worthy of being picked by Thermal Grizzly as a delidded option, and it's even cheaper than a stock 285K
One for the serious clockers only but I still kinda want one.

author: PC Gamer
text:
'Going just 0.5 mm thicker would probably have saved me months of engineering': Redditor crafts actual credit card sized computer
Imagine saying 'here's my card' with this.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Star Wars: The Old Republic's original director hadn't played a single MMO before launching its development studio: 'How the hell did they trust me with this much money?'
"The next day, I created a WoW character."

author: PC Gamer
text:
How to get Celestine in Subnautica 2
Grab this rare material so you craft Strontium.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Hear the Steam Controller sing whole songs with haptics, including the Wii Store soundtrack, Still Alive, and even attempt a Rick Roll
This was a triumph. I'm making a note here, 'huge success'.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Stardew Valley creator Eric Barone would never use AI, instead wants to rely on human creativity—'I feel like that should take priority over a soulless machine'
Expressing yourself should always be at the centre of all things creative.

author: PC Gamer
text:
How to get Creature Enamel in Subnautica 2
You'll need this material to craft Enameled Glass.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Final Fantasy 14 is promising big changes with Evercold, but I hope it's not a surface-level attempt
The MMO needs to break free from its predictive chains.

author: PC Gamer
text:
How to get a fully functional Fabricator in Subnautica 2
It really is that simple.

author: PC Gamer
text:
'Average Valve W': After some Steam Controller packages were sent to the wrong country, Valve is reportedly giving 'any standard-edition title' to affected shoppers
Apparently, a small number of packages were accidentally sent to the UK.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Alienware's new 'budget' laptop is a damning indictment of the current state of PC gaming hardware
The Alienware 15 is a new RTX 3050 gaming laptop in 2026. With resolutely single-channel memory.

author: PC Gamer
text:
How to make Titanium Ingots in Subnautica 2
You won't find this material in the ocean, as you've got to make it yourself.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Don't sleep on Battlestar Galactica: Scattered Hopes—it's tricky, tactical and the best FTL-like since, well, FTL
So say we all.

author: PC Gamer
text:
15% of PC Gamer readers are still rawdogging their desktops, while 43% of you are wholesome full desk pad people
Do you use a mouse mat while gaming?

author: PC Gamer
text:
Framework says its Wireless Touchpad Keyboard 'is by far our most waitlisted product'
Might be a bit galling for a company that's better known for making laptops.

author: PC Gamer
text:
We're getting closer to a 100% ROG rig as Asus gets into the DRAM game for its 20th anniversary
It might just be a one-off, though.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Forza Horizon 6's return to 'wristband progression' isn't the stand-in for a career mode I wanted it to be, but maybe that's for the best
Be car-ful what you wish for.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Rig R5 Spear Max HD gaming headset review
A very interesting wired headset, with some tuning I can't quite get my head around.

author: PC Gamer
text:
After close to 140 hours with it, I think Slay the Spire 2 feels more like a sequel than a DLC, even if Megacrit was forced to make it
Not just a shuffle of the deck.

author: PC Gamer
text:
I was worried EverQuest Legends making me too OP would ruin the magic of the classic MMO, but crushing hordes of frogloks is incredibly satisfying
Friction is great, but triple-classing isn't so bad, either.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Despite Donald Trump and Jensen Huang flying out to the country, China has not yet approved the sale of Nvidia AI chips
This is after Trump turned up in China to increase AI trade.

author: PC Gamer
text:
I really hope you weren't considering a GPU upgrade this year, as Nvidia may raise the price of the already pricey RTX 5090 by as much as $300
Memory is proving an expensive problem.

author: PC Gamer
text:
'I just need to disappear, go into a cabin': Eric Barone has found the answer to Haunted Chocolatier's development distractions
Barone has dedicated five days a week to Haunted Chocolatier.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Hot on the heels of Apple's iPhone-in-a-laptop, Google and Intel have teamed up to give us the Googlebook
No word on the hardware yet, but Panther or Wildcat Lake are the most likely options.

author: PC Gamer
text:
How to get the Repair Tool in Subnautica 2
Repair your vehicles, bases, and facilities with one click.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Sketch group Aunty Donna's new parody of Black Mirror's Bandersnatch episode will send you on a 40-minute unskippable walk around Melbourne if you make the wrong choices
Bandersketch invites you to help the boys make their next video... as long as you can make the right choices.

author: PC Gamer
text:
An engineer has worked out how to speed up a 3D printer by 1000% with a Nintendo Switch, though 'for most people, a Raspberry Pi is a better choice'
That's one way to fight e-waste.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Intel's 270K Plus is worthy of being picked by Thermal Grizzly as a delidded option, and it's even cheaper than a stock 285K
One for the serious clockers only but I still kinda want one.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Another metaverse is set to die in June
Holoearth, which just launched in 2024, is the latest proof that PC Gamer's Wes Fenlon was right.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Diablo 4 players become unkillable gods as a minor bug fix in Lord of Hatred turns into a major problem for Blizzard
A second bug with defensive items has given several classes the ability to become effectively immortal.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Valve's massive update to the Steam marketplace must be working, because a gun I had listed on there for years just sold for a cool 40 bucks
The only thing better than an item listing is a bigger item listing with more graphs.

author: PC Gamer
text:
The world of videogames is 'currently not well understood' with major decisions based on 'a limited, partial view', say industry organisations UKIE and OKRE
They've launched a new report calling for a more comprehensive and unified research framework.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Notorious videogame movie auteur Uwe Boll is returning to the director's chair with a spiritual successor to House of the Dead
Boll heard Paul W.S. Anderson is doing an official House of the Dead reboot for Sega, and he's not having it.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Baldur's Gate 2 almost had a 'time travel plot', letting you visit an alternate future where BG1's main villain rules the Sword Coast
Sadly Sarevok never got his do-over.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Framework says its Wireless Touchpad Keyboard 'is by far our most waitlisted product'
